seoTitle: Adjust Table Cell Width & Height in Google Docs
metaDescription: Resize table rows and columns in Google Docs by dragging boundaries, entering exact dimensions, or using the Distribute Rows/Columns commands.
intro: Uneven table cells make reports and schedules look sloppy. Google Docs gives you both a quick drag-and-drop method and a precise numerical way to resize rows and columns, plus one-click commands to make everything uniform. This guide covers all three.

steps.heading: Drag boundary lines for quick changes
steps.body: Click and drag row or column boundary lines directly to make fast visual adjustments to cell size.

steps.heading: Open Table Properties for precise control
steps.body: Right-click inside the table and open Table Properties to access the advanced sizing sidebar.

steps.heading: Enter exact width and height values
steps.body: Overwrite the default dimensions with specific numerical values to achieve uniform column and row sizing.

steps.heading: Use Distribute Rows and Distribute Columns
steps.body: Run these automation commands to instantly standardize sizing across the whole table structure.

steps.heading: Adjust cell padding
steps.body: Use the formatting panel's padding controls to fine-tune the spacing between text and cell borders.

faq.question: How do I make all my table columns the same width?
faq.answer: Use the Distribute Columns command to instantly equalize column widths across the table.

faq.question: Can I set an exact cell size instead of dragging?
faq.answer: Yes, Table Properties lets you type in precise numerical width and height values.

faq.question: Why does my table push content onto a new page?
faq.answer: Inconsistent cell sizing can cause structural overflow, so keeping row heights balanced helps prevent rows from spilling onto separate pages.

faq.question: Can I control the space between text and cell borders?
faq.answer: Yes, custom cell padding settings in the formatting panel let you adjust that spacing directly.
